mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 7 Series engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned BMW 7 Series engine typically costs £2,800–£10,000+ supply only [BMW-QUOTE] — from the B47 diesel at £2,800–£4,500 to the N63 V8 at £7,000–£10,000+. The N74 V12 (760i) costs £8,000–£14,000+; the S68 V8 (G70 760i) costs £9,000–£15,000. Fitting adds £650–£2,500. Use our calculator for a personalised figure.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 7 Series engine is the most reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: The B58B30 straight-six in the G11/G12/G70 740i — the most dependable modern BMW engine in our dataset and the best engine ever fitted to a 7 Series. From the classic era, the M57D30 in the E38/E65 730d is one of the most durable diesel engines ever built.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 7 Series engine should I avoid?

acceptedAnswer.text: No engine outright — but the N63B44 V8 (F01/G11 750i) without a documented timing chain guide replacement, valve stem seal history, and comprehensive warranty is the one purchase we consistently advise against. The N62 V8 (E65 750i) carries the same timing chain guide risk. A B58 740i has none of these caveats.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my 7 Series engine needs replacing?

acceptedAnswer.text: By engine: rattling on cold start (N63/M62/N62 timing chain guides), blue smoke on startup and excessive oil consumption (N63/N62 valve stem seals), oil in coolant (N63 turbo failure), long cranking and limp mode (N63 HPFP failure). On the M57, cooling system failure is the main cause of engine death — watch for coolant loss. Caught early, it's a repair; ignored, it's a £2,800–£10,000+ engine [BMW-QUOTE]. Use our diagnostic calculator for a personalised assessment.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 7 Series engine?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends entirely on generation and engine. Against a £70,000 G70 760i, a £9,000–£15,000 S68 recon is 13–21% — ✅ safe. Against a £20,000 G11 750i, a £7,000–£10,000 N63 recon is 35–50% — ⚠️ watch. Against an £8,000 F01 750i, a £7,000–£10,000+ recon is 88–125% — ❌ walk away. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Use our calculator for your car.

mainEntity.name: Which BMW 7 Series should I buy?

acceptedAnswer.text: G11/G12 740i with the B58 — the most dependable 7 Series ever made, 326 bhp, 40+ mpg, and depreciated into sensible territory. For diesel, G11/G12 730d with B57 — 50+ mpg and bulletproof. For the classic, E38 730d with M57 — the most beautiful 7 Series and one of the most dependable engines ever made. Avoid F01 750i without a comprehensive warranty; the B58 is the safe choice.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 7 Series engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and in the vehicle documentation; block stamping is visible from below on most engines. Or tell us your generation, model year and variant (730d, 740i, 750i, 760i) and we'll identify it — 20+ vetted specialists will confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: BMW 7 Series vs 5 Series — which is more reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: Mechanically they share engines across the same eras — the N52/N62/N63/B57/B58 story is identical. The 7 Series is heavier and more complex (especially the long-wheelbase and V12 variants), so it works the powertrain harder. Buy on engine and year, not badge. The 5 Series offers more value; the 7 Series offers more presence and luxury.

hero.h1: 7 Series Engines — The Complete UK Guide
hero.subHeadline: Every BMW 7 Series engine across seven generations — from the carburetted M30 six-cylinder to the N63 twin-turbo V8 and the electrified B58 of the G70. Real UK replacement costs, known failure patterns, and honest ownership economics for BMW's flagship. Backed by 300+ real UK 7 Series engine enquiries in 2025 [BMW-VERIFIED].

metrics.ourCall: ⚠️ The 7 Series rule — flagship bills, bargain-bin values: a £7,000–£10,000+ N63 recon against a £8,000 F01 750i is 88–125% of the car's value — ❌ scrap threshold. Against a £70,000 G70 760i, the same repair is just 10–14% — ✅ safe. Generation decides everything.
★★★☆☆ — the M30 and M57 are bulletproof; the N62 and N63 V8s define the risk; the B58 and B57 are the modern gold standard.
B58B30 (G70 740i) and M57D30 (E38/E65 730d) — the modern and classic straight-six diesels
N63B44 (F01 750i) — timing chain guides, valve stem seals, turbo failures, HPFP • N62B48 (E65 750i) — timing chain guides, valve stem seals
G11/G12 740i with B58 — the modern straight-six in a depreciated flagship chassis, no V8 caveats
G11/G12 730d with B57 — 50+ mpg, 600-mile range, and the most dependable 7 Series powertrain
N63 timing chain guides / valve stem seals — shared-platform data [BMW-VERIFIED]
👑 The Flagship — BMW's technology leader and the most expensive engine bills in the non-M BMW range

ownershipVerdict.oneLineVerdict: The 7 Series is BMW's flagship — the car that introduces every new technology before any other model. The engines range from bulletproof sixes to V8s that can cost more to replace than the car is worth. Buy the straight-six unless you have the budget for V8 bills — and on an N63, a documented timing chain and valve seal history is worth more than any service stamp.

H2: BMW 7 Series Generations — Find Your Vehicle

rows.metric: Sub-headline: Seven generations, nearly 50 years, and one flagship nameplate that defines BMW luxury. The right 7 Series depends entirely on which generation you're willing to maintain.

Our Verdict: The original flagship — the car that created the modern BMW luxury sedan. M30 six-cylinders are bulletproof; the M102 turbo is rare and complex. Bodywork and electronics are the real projects now.

rows.value: Our Verdict: The first 7 Series with a V8 and V12 — the M60's Nikasil liner issues are the caveat on early cars; post-1994 M60s are robust. The M70 V12 is a masterpiece but carries twin-bank maintenance costs.
BMW 7 Series E38 🔥 Most Enquired — classic era [BMW-VERIFIED]

rows.metric: Our Verdict: The 7 Series at its most beautiful — and the first to offer a diesel. The M57 730d is one of the most dependable BMW engines ever made; the M62 V8's timing chain guides are the defining risk. The 728i with M52 is the robust petrol choice.
BMW 7 Series E65/E66 🔥 Most Enquired — 300+ enquiries (2025) [BMW-VERIFIED]

rows.value: Our Verdict: The controversial Bangle-era 7 Series — and the N62 V8's timing chain guides and valve stem seals define the risk. The 730d with M57 is the sensible pick; the V8s carry the caveat; the 760i with N73 V12 is a masterpiece with twin-bank costs.
BMW 7 Series F01/F02 🔥 Most Enquired — F01 750i drives N63 volume [BMW-VERIFIED]

rows.metric: Our Verdict: The generation that brought twin-turbo V8s to the 7 Series — and the N63's timing chain guides, valve stem seals and turbo failures define the risk. The 730d with M57/N57 is the robust choice; the 750i's N63 is the one to avoid without a warranty.

rows.value: Our Verdict: The CLAR-platform 7 Series — and the most dependable modern 7 Series ever made. The 740i with B58 is the sweet spot; the 730d with B57 is the diesel perfection. The N63 in the 750i still carries the V8 caveat; the M760i's V12 is the flagship experience.

rows.metric: 2022–present • B58 (735i/740i), B57 (740d), S68 V8 (760i), electric i7

Our Verdict: The seventh-generation 7 Series — available with petrol, diesel, plug-in hybrid, and fully electric i7 powertrains. The B58 in the 740i is as robust as ever; the S68 V8 in the 760i is BMW's latest twin-turbo V8; the i7 is the future. Too new for volume reliability data, but the B-family record is impeccable.
The V8 Divide — The Line That Matters Most
Timing chain guides + valve stem seals + turbo failure

rows.value: Buy only with documented guide replacement

engineDatabase.h2: BMW 7 Series Engine Codes — The Complete Database
engineDatabase.subHeadline: Every engine ever fitted to a BMW 7 Series — verified codes, specifications and real UK ownership data.

confidenceScore.title: How confident are these ratings?
confidenceScore.text: N63B44, B58B30, B57D30, M57D30 ratings: 90% confidence — high-volume engines with deep specialist rebuild history and clear failure patterns from BMW TSB data and 100+ specialist interviews. N62/M62 ratings: 85% confidence — well-documented timing chain guide failure patterns. E23/E32/E38 engines: Expert-knowledge tier — decades of documented patterns and enthusiast records, not proprietary volume data. G70 engines: Provisional — the parc is too young for volume data; ratings reflect early patterns and shared-architecture evidence. Data note (internal) — corrections applied: Correction applied: The N63B44 "hot-vee" V8 is the defining risk of the F01 7 Series — timing chain guides made of brittle plastic break apart, sending metal fragments through the engine. High oil consumption from worn valve stem seals leads to lubrication starvation. Turbochargers fail from excessive heat. BMW's later N63TU updates address some problems while creating new ones. Independent mechanics report repair bills consistently exceeding £7,000–£10,000. Correction applied: The M62 V8 (E38 735i/740i) timing chain guides are the defining failure — plastic guides shatter, sending debris through the oil system. Symptoms typically begin between 80,000 and 100,000 miles. Correction applied: The "740i" badge covers four different engines across generations — M62 V8 (E38), N62 V8 (E65), N54 straight-six (F01), B58 straight-six (G11/G70). The engine family — not the badge — determines reliability. Correction applied: The G70 7 Series (2022+) offers petrol (B58, S68), diesel (B57), plug-in hybrid (750e, M760e) and fully electric (i7) powertrains. The B58 in the 740i produces 381 PS; the S68 V8 in the 760i produces 544 PS.

commonProblems.subHeadline: The six most common failure patterns across the 7 Series range — symptoms, costs and urgency.

problems.description: The defining F01 7 Series issue — plastic timing chain guides break apart, sending metal fragments through the engine. Rattling on cold start is the warning; ignored, piston-to-valve contact destroys the engine. Affects F01 750i (2008–2015). £7,000–£10,000+ recon.

problems.issue: N63 Valve Stem Seal & Oil Consumption
problems.description: The N63's companion failure — worn valve stem seals cause blue smoke on startup and excessive oil consumption (1L per 1,000 miles). Affects F01 750i. Often requires engine-out repair.

The E38 and E65 V8s' defining failure — plastic timing chain guides shatter, sending debris through the oil system. Rattling on cold start is the warning. Symptoms typically begin at 80,000–100,000 miles. £3,000–£6,000 repair.
The N63's hot-vee design causes turbocharger failure from excessive heat and poor oil return. High-pressure fuel pumps dump raw fuel into cylinders, destroying pistons and rods. Affects F01 750i.

Not an engine failure — but the most common non-engine 7 Series enquiry. The E38 and E65's air suspension and complex electronics (iDrive, ABS modules) are costly to repair. Check compressor and suspension on any E38/E65.

The modern B58's known weak point — coolant levels drop suddenly in the primary reservoir, leading to overheating. Valve cover gaskets can fail due to overheating. Affects G11/G70 740i (2015+). £400–£800 repair, not catastrophic if caught early.

marketIntelligence.h2: BMW 7 Series Market Intelligence — What 300+ UK Owners Told Us in 2025

signals.demandTrend: ⬆ Rising — the N63's reputation drives due-diligence searches [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — B58 cars entering affordable range [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
⬆ Rising — new generation entering used market [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]
➡ Stable — the N63's reputation is well-known [PLACEHOLDER — YoY %]

insights: Interesting insight: The 7 Series generates fewer enquiries than the 5 Series — but the enquiries it does generate are significantly more expensive. The N63 V8 drives the majority of the volume, with owners facing £7,000–£10,000+ recon bills on cars worth £8,000–£20,000. The B58 owners, by contrast, barely feature in the data — the B58 is proving as robust in the flagship as it is in the 3 Series. (Editorial synthesis — specialist sense-check before publishing.)

F01 750i owners are the core of the 7 Series enquiry data — and the N63's timing chain guides, valve stem seals, turbo failures and HPFP issues are the primary drivers. These cars are now entering affordable territory (£8,000–£15,000 for a 750i), but the £7,000–£10,000+ recon bill means buyers need to budget accordingly. The 730d with M57/N57 is the sensible choice; the 750i is the emotional one — and the expensive one. (Editorial synthesis.)

The G11/G12 740i with the B58 is the 7 Series to buy — the B58 is one of the most reliable BMW engines ever built, the G11 chassis is sharp, and depreciation has already done its work. The G70 is too new for volume reliability data, but the B-family record is impeccable. (Editorial synthesis.)

editorialPullQuote.title: The One Thing We'd Tell Every BMW 7 Series Buyer
editorialPullQuote.quote: If you buy one 7 Series based on this page, buy a G11/G12 740i with the B58 straight-six — it's the most dependable engine ever fitted to the flagship, delivers 326 bhp and 40+ mpg, and the G11 chassis is the sharpest 7 Series ever made. If the V8 is calling you, budget £7,000–£10,000+ for an N63 recon and buy the best warranty you can find. And on an E38 or E65 V8, a documented timing chain guide replacement is worth more than any service stamp.

replacementCosts.h2: BMW 7 Series Engine Replacement Costs — By Generation
replacementCosts.subHeadline: Real UK pricing for used, reconditioned and rebuilt engines — supply only, with labour guidance.
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Labour estimate: Fitting a BMW 7 Series engine typically takes 10–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The 7 Series rule — flagship bills, bargain-bin values: A £7,000–£10,000+ N63 recon against an £8,000 F01 750i sits at 88–125% of the car's value — ❌ scrap threshold. Against a £70,000 G70 760i, a £9,000–£15,000 S68 recon is just 13–21% — ✅ safe. Against a £5,000 E38 740i, a £3,000–£6,000 M62 recon is 60–120% — ⚠️ watch. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Value the car first, then the engine. The B58 740i is the modern sweet spot — reliable, economical, and the flagship experience without the V8 bills.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a BMW 7 Series engine typically takes 10–24 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer) [BMW-QUOTE]. The 7 Series rule — flagship bills, bargain-bin values: A £7,000–£10,000+ N63 recon against an £8,000 F01 750i sits at 88–125% of the car's value — ❌ scrap threshold. Against a £70,000 G70 760i, a £9,000–£15,000 S68 recon is just 13–21% — ✅ safe. Against a £5,000 E38 740i, a £3,000–£6,000 M62 recon is 60–120% — ⚠️ watch. Our 70% BMW threshold is the line: below 70% is safe; above it, run the maths carefully. Value the car first, then the engine. The B58 740i is the modern sweet spot — reliable, economical, and the flagship experience without the V8 bills.

engineEvolution.h2: How the BMW 7 Series Engine Range Evolved — 1977 to Today

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW's first flagship sedan launched with the M30 six-cylinder — the same engine family that powered the 3 Series. The M102 turbo arrived in 1980 for the 745i.
eras.worthKnowing: The M30 is one of BMW's most durable engines — 200k+ miles is routine. The M102 turbo is rare and complex.

eras.whyBmwChanged: BMW introduced the M60 V8 (first V8 in a BMW since the 1960s) and the M70 V12 — a landmark engineering achievement.
eras.worthKnowing: The M60's Nikasil liners suffered from high-sulphur fuel issues on early cars — buy with a liner test or post-1994 build. The M70 V12 is a masterpiece but carries twin-bank maintenance costs.

eras.keyEngines: M52/M54, M62 V8, M57 diesel, M73 V12
eras.whyBmwChanged: The E38 introduced the M57 diesel — the first diesel 7 Series — and refined the V8 with the M62. The M73 V12 replaced the M70.
eras.worthKnowing: The M57 is one of BMW's most dependable diesel engines — regularly exceeding 250,000 miles. The M62 V8's timing chain guides are the defining risk.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The Bangle-era 7 Series brought the N62 V8 — BMW's first V8 with variable valve lift and electric throttle. The N73 V12 replaced the M73.
eras.worthKnowing: The N62's timing chain guides and valve stem seals are the defining failures. The M57 730d remains the sensible choice.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The F01 introduced twin-turbocharging across the range — the N54 straight-six in the 740i, the N63 V8 in the 750i, the N57 diesel in the 730d/740d.
eras.worthKnowing: The N63 V8 is the defining risk — timing chain guides, valve stem seals, turbo failures, HPFP issues. The N57 diesel is the robust choice.

eras.whyBmwChanged: The CLAR-platform G11 brought B-family engines — the B58 straight-six in the 740i, the B57 diesel in the 730d/740d. The N63 was updated to the N63TU but still carries V8 caveats.
eras.worthKnowing: The B58 is the most dependable 7 Series powertrain ever made — no V8 chain issues, no diesel DPF nightmares. The 740i is the G11 to buy.

eras.keyEngines: B58, B57, S68 V8, i7 electric
eras.whyBmwChanged: The seventh-generation 7 Series — available with petrol, diesel, plug-in hybrid, and fully electric i7 powertrains. The i7 is the future; the B58 is the combustion gold standard.
eras.worthKnowing: The G70 is too new for volume reliability data, but the B-family record is impeccable. The S68 V8 in the 760i is BMW's latest twin-turbo V8 — too early to assess long-term reliability.

whoShouldBuy.h2: Who Should Buy a BMW 7 Series?

profiles.verdict: The 7 Series is built for this — the G11/G12 740i with the B58 delivers flagship luxury, 326 bhp, 40+ mpg, and the most dependable engine ever fitted to the 7 Series. The G70 740i is the modern version. Buy the B58; avoid the V8s unless you have a warranty.

The 7 Series is a flagship, not a commuter car — it's long, wide, and thirsty (especially the V8s). The 730d with B57 is the most sensible choice, but the 5 Series does the commuting job better. Buy a 730d if you must, but the 5 Series is the sensible choice.

The most dangerous badge on this site for a tight budget. A £6,000 F01 750i is not a cheap 7 Series — it's an expensive one with the first year's bills prepaid by nobody. A £15,000 G11 740i with B58 is the bargain; a £6,000 750i without a warranty is the trap.

The 7 Series Long Wheelbase (E66/F02/G12/G70) is the definitive chauffeur car — acres of rear legroom, reclining seats, and the quietest cabin in the range. The B57 diesel is the sensible choice; the V8s are for clients who don't pay the fuel bills.

An E38 730d with the M57 is a rising classic — the most beautiful 7 Series and one of the most dependable diesel engines ever made. A G11 740i with the B58 is the modern keeper — reliable, beautiful, and appreciating as the last ICE 7 Series approaches.

calculatorCta.h2: Can I Repair My BMW 7 Series Engine — or Should I Replace It?
calculatorCta.intro: Rattling on cold start from an N63? Blue smoke on startup from an N62? On the 7 Series, the answer swings on your car's value — an £8,000 F01 and a £70,000 G70 face very different economics. Get a personalised repair-vs-replace verdict using real UK cost data.
